$comment: Example 3: Full complexity — migrate a monolith API to serverless with zero downtime

intent.goal_statement: Migrate the monolithic REST API from EC2 to AWS Lambda + API Gateway with zero downtime
intent.motivation: Current EC2 deployment has scaling issues during traffic spikes and high idle costs outside peak hours. Serverless will reduce costs by ~60% and auto-scale.
intent.success_definition: 100% of API traffic is served by Lambda functions through API Gateway with no client-visible downtime and p95 latency within 200ms of current baseline
intent.scope_context: Express.js monolith deployed on EC2 behind ALB, using PostgreSQL (RDS) and Redis (ElastiCache). 47 API endpoints across 8 domains.

in_scope: Extracting 47 endpoints from Express monolith into Lambda functions
Setting up API Gateway with Lambda proxy integration
Blue-green traffic shifting via weighted target groups
Observability: CloudWatch metrics, X-Ray tracing, structured logging
CI/CD pipeline updates for Lambda deployment
out_of_scope: Database migration (staying on RDS PostgreSQL, not moving to DynamoDB)
API contract changes (endpoints must maintain backward compatibility)
Frontend changes (API URLs remain the same via custom domain)
Authentication/authorization redesign (existing JWT middleware ported as-is)
Decomposing into microservices — this is a lift-and-shift, endpoints map 1:1 to functions
assumptions: RDS Proxy is available in the target region
Lambda concurrency limits can be raised via quota increase if needed
Existing integration tests cover >70% of API surface
ALB can route weighted traffic to both EC2 and Lambda targets simultaneously

approach.overview: Strangler-fig migration: extract endpoints domain by domain (8 domains total), deploy as Lambda functions behind API Gateway, shift traffic incrementally (10% → 50% → 100% per domain), validate, and decommission EC2.
approach.rationale: Strangler-fig allows incremental migration with rollback capability at each step. Domain-by-domain extraction keeps each migration unit reviewable and limits blast radius.

alternatives_considered.approach: Big-bang: deploy all endpoints to Lambda and cut over via DNS
alternatives_considered.rejection_reason: High risk — no incremental validation, rollback requires DNS TTL wait

alternatives_considered.approach: Container migration to ECS Fargate instead of Lambda
alternatives_considered.rejection_reason: Fargate doesn't solve the idle-cost problem as effectively as Lambda for spiky traffic patterns; team has AWS Lambda expertise

alternatives_considered.approach: Full decomposition into independently deployable microservices
alternatives_considered.rejection_reason: Scope is too large for this migration; decompose after serverless infrastructure is proven

steps.title: Provision Lambda infrastructure via Terraform
steps.description: Create Terraform modules for: Lambda function template, API Gateway HTTP API, RDS Proxy, CloudWatch log groups, X-Ray sampling rule, IAM roles with least-privilege policies.
steps.rationale: IaC is required for reproducible environments and rollback capability
steps.inputs: Current AWS account topology, existing Terraform state
steps.acceptance_criteria: Terraform plan succeeds; staging Lambda can be deployed; RDS Proxy connects to RDS; X-Ray traces appear in console

steps.title: Create Lambda adapter for Express middleware
steps.description: Build a serverless-express or custom adapter that translates API Gateway events to Express request/response, preserving JWT middleware, error handling, and request logging.
steps.rationale: Without an adapter, every endpoint needs manual rewriting — adapter enables near-verbatim port
steps.acceptance_criteria: Adapter correctly parses API Gateway proxy events; Express middleware chain executes; response is correctly formatted for API Gateway

steps.title: Migrate Domain 1-2 (auth, users) — pilot domains
steps.description: Extract auth (8 endpoints) and users (6 endpoints) domains. Deploy as Lambda functions. Configure API Gateway routes. Set up weighted routing: 10% Lambda / 90% EC2. Monitor for 48 hours, then shift to 50/50, then 100%.
steps.rationale: Auth and users are the most traffic-heavy domains — validates the pattern under real load and tests DB connection pooling
steps.inputs: Lambda adapter from s2, Terraform modules from s1
steps.outputs: Two Lambda-deployed domains with traffic shifting config
steps.acceptance_criteria: p95 latency within 200ms of EC2 baseline for migrated endpoints; error rate < 0.1%; traffic shifting works correctly at 10/50/100 weights; rollback (shift back to EC2) works in < 60 seconds

steps.title: Update CI/CD pipeline for Lambda deployments
steps.description: Extend the existing GitHub Actions workflow to: build Lambda zip artifacts, upload to S3, trigger Lambda update via AWS CLI, run smoke tests against the new function version.
steps.rationale: Pipeline must support Lambda-specific deployment steps alongside existing EC2 pipeline
steps.outputs: Updated pipeline with Lambda deployment stage
steps.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline builds and deploys a Lambda function update end-to-end; smoke tests run against the updated function; pipeline fails fast on deployment errors

steps.title: Migrate Domains 3-5 (orders, payments, inventory)
steps.description: Apply the proven pattern from the pilot to orders (7 endpoints), payments (5 endpoints), and inventory (4 endpoints). These domains can be migrated in parallel since they share no stateful dependencies.
steps.rationale: Batching these three domains accelerates the migration timeline while staying within risk tolerance
steps.inputs: Validated pattern from s3, CI/CD from s4
steps.acceptance_criteria: Each domain passes the same criteria as s3; no cross-domain regressions detected

steps.title: Migrate Domains 6-8 (notifications, search, admin)
steps.description: Extract remaining domains: notifications (5 endpoints), search (7 endpoints), admin (5 endpoints). Admin endpoints require human-gated deployment due to sensitive permissions.
steps.rationale: These domains have lower traffic but admin has elevated sensitivity
steps.acceptance_criteria: All endpoints migrated; full API surface returns correct responses; admin endpoints audited for permission correctness

steps.title: Configure weighted DNS routing for full cutover
steps.description: Update Route 53 / API Gateway custom domain to route 100% of API traffic through API Gateway. Keep EC2 instances warm for 72 hours as hot standby.
steps.rationale: Hot standby provides rapid rollback capability during the final cutover window
steps.outputs: DNS configuration pointing to API Gateway
steps.acceptance_criteria: 100% of production traffic served by Lambda; EC2 standby is idle; DNS TTL is low (< 60s) for rollback; traffic dashboard confirms zero client-visible errors

steps.title: Decommission EC2 infrastructure
steps.description: After 72 hours of stable Lambda operation, terminate EC2 instances, remove ALB, release EIPs, and update Terraform to remove EC2 resources. Archive old deployment artifacts.
steps.rationale: Decommissioning completes the migration and removes idle costs
steps.inputs: 72 hours of stable production traffic on Lambda (s7)
steps.outputs: Cleaned-up AWS account, updated Terraform state
steps.acceptance_criteria: EC2 instances terminated; ALB removed; monthly AWS bill projection reflects ~60% compute cost reduction; Terraform state is clean

constraints.description: Zero downtime — no request may fail due to migration activity
constraints.validation_method: Synthetic monitoring on all endpoints during migration windows; alert on any 5xx spike

constraints.description: p95 latency for migrated endpoints must be within 200ms of the EC2 baseline
constraints.validation_method: CloudWatch latency comparison between EC2 and Lambda for same endpoints

constraints.description: IAM roles for Lambda must follow least-privilege — no wildcard permissions
constraints.validation_method: Automated IAM policy linting in CI/CD pipeline

constraints.description: PII logging must be explicitly disabled in Lambda log groups
constraints.validation_method: Log sample review + automated PII pattern scanner on CloudWatch logs

constraints.description: RDS Proxy must be used for all database connections from Lambda
constraints.validation_method: Database connection count stays stable during load test (no connection storms)

success_criteria.description: 100% of API traffic is served by Lambda through API Gateway
No client-visible downtime during migration (zero failed requests attributable to migration)
p95 latency is within 200ms of pre-migration baseline
Monthly compute cost is reduced by ≥ 50% (target: ~60%)

risks.title: Cold start latency on first requests after scaling to zero
risks.description: Lambda functions scaling from 0 to 1 concurrent execution add 1-3s latency on the first request
risks.mitigation: Enable provisioned concurrency for high-traffic domains (auth, users, orders); set minimum concurrency to 5 during business hours
risks.contingency: If provisioned concurrency cost exceeds savings, evaluate scheduled warming or revert affected domains to EC2

risks.title: RDS Proxy connection limits under high concurrency
risks.description: Lambda can spawn hundreds of concurrent connections; even with RDS Proxy, we could hit database connection limits
risks.mitigation: Set Lambda reserved concurrency to cap max parallel executions; monitor database connection count during load tests
risks.contingency: Raise RDS max_connections or add read replica if connection pressure persists

risks.title: Incompatible Express middleware that doesn't translate to Lambda
risks.description: Some middleware (e.g., long-lived WebSockets, server-sent events) may not work in Lambda's request/response model
risks.mitigation: Audit all middleware during pilot domain migration (s3); identify and exclude incompatible endpoints
risks.contingency: Keep incompatible endpoints on EC2 or migrate to ECS Fargate as a hybrid approach

risks.title: Terraform state corruption during incremental migration
risks.description: Long-running incremental migration means Terraform state spans many changes; drift or corruption could occur
risks.mitigation: Use S3 backend with DynamoDB locking; run terraform plan in CI before every apply; audit state weekly
risks.contingency: Restore from Terraform state backup; manually reconcile drift

dependencies.description: AWS quota increase for Lambda concurrent executions (request: 1000)
RDS Proxy available in target region (us-east-1)
Route 53 hosted zone for API domain
Existing integration test suite covering API surface

step_overrides.rationale: Admin endpoints have elevated permissions — human must review IAM roles and access patterns
DNS cutover affects all production traffic — human authorization required
Decommissioning is irreversible — human sign-off required

revision_history.change_summary: Initial migration plan: all-at-once Lambda deployment
Revised to strangler-fig approach after team feedback on blast radius

changes.summary: Changed from big-bang to strangler-fig incremental migration
Added parallel domain migration batch (orders, payments, inventory)

revision_history.change_summary: Added RDS Proxy dependency, provisioned concurrency mitigation for cold starts, PII logging constraint

changes.summary: Added provisioned concurrency to cold start mitigation
